titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/149

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=421 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:12:17.339022+00 | translation moa=422 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:12:18.855941+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis  Vctredus usulfi salutem . Sciant tam posteri quam presentes . quod ego vctredus bono consensu et assensu Thome filii mei et heredis .
pro redemptione anime domini mei regis . Malcolmi . . . us . et pro salute anime meę et filiorum meorum . do et concedo ęcclesię Sancte Marie de maylros . et monachis ibidem
deo seruientibus Ringuude . liberam et quietam ab omni terreno seruitio . et assisis . et geldis . et auxiliis . et omni seculari exactione et consuetudine . in perpetuam ele
mosinam . in bosco . in plano . in pratis . . . . et in pascuis . per has diuisas . scilicet ab eo loco quo alewent cadit in Teuiet . et inde sursum usque ad blachapol . et ita ad
bollinnes burne . et sic inde ad . . . et inde recte in transuersum usque pennango . et a pennango recte in transuersum . . . ad Alewente . et inde sursum ad bru
nemore super dod . et sic usque ad . . . usque ubi illa burna cadit in alewente . Preterea do et concedo eis hanc libertatem . ut nec ego nec alius uenetur
infra predictas diuisas  nisi per ipsos . . . o abbate Rieuall’ . Thoma filio meo . Adam de dunbar . Willelmo frater eius . Waltero clerico de ercheldune . Thoma
clerico de uuittun . Gamello clerico . . . Adam de stanford . hugone de cole . Edmundo auunculo vctredi . Gospatricio filio aldani . Radulfo filio vctredi . Eiulfo ruffo . Edmundo . . . Willelmo de ireuude .

p.cl: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, Uhtred son of Osulf sends greeting.

Let those to come as well as those present know that I, Uhtred, by the kind consent and assent of Thomas my son and heir, for the redemption of the soul of my lord King Malcolm . . . and for the salvation of my own soul and of my sons', I give and grant to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, Ringwood, free and quit from every earthly service, assessments, gelds, and aids, and every secular exaction and custom, in everlasting alms, in wood, in plain, in meadows . . . and in pastures; by these boundaries, that is, from that place at which the Alewent falls into the Teviot, and from there upwards to Blachapol, and so to Bollines burn, and so from there to . . . and from there straight across to Pennango, and from Pennango straight across as far as Alewent, and from there upwards to Brunemore on the Dod, and so to . . . as far as where that burn falls into the Alewent. In addition I give and grant them this liberty, that neither I nor another hunt within the said boundaries, unless by their [permission.

As witnesses: Ailred] abbot of Rievaulx; Thomas my son; Adam of Dunbar; William his brother; Walter, clerk of Earlston; Thomas, clerk of Whitton; Gamel, clerk . . . ; Adam of Stanford; Hugh of Cole; Edmund the uncle of Uhtred; Gospatric son of Haldane; Ralph son of Uhtred; Eiulf Ruffus; Edmund . . . ; William of Erwood.
